cells.rationale: Touches the resume identity contract and byte-pinned launch argv where a stale env carrier propagates past the task, so it needs contract-shaped reasoning.
Keystone inversion introducing a new resolver module with defensive torn-tail parsing and multiple typed failure modes, high uncertainty and blast radius.
Consolidates duplicated selection logic and re-owns generation identity (a canonical contract) while preserving a DB index path, a wrong abstraction here forks boots.
Carries the escalate-or-refuse contract plus a clobber guard whose failure silently loses the pre-crash restore mirror, correctness-sensitive beyond its surface.
Largest task: a verified-restore state machine with flock idempotency, crash-loop bounds, evidence reads, and byte-pins where a false verified corrupts trust.
Single-file event-sourced fold guard with a named root cause and precise spec, but re-fold determinism and projection poisoning keep it on the strongest model.
New CLI verb reusing the existing back-fill seam along a clear path, but the heuristic proximity matching risks becoming a poisoning vector so honesty/ambiguity handling needs strong judgment.
Test instruments plus a docs sweep following existing slow-test conventions, but the hermetic real-tmux e2e with a fake harness carries real leaked-launch risk warranting careful execution.

verdict_raw:
All self-checks pass (schema_version 1, ids match, non-empty axes/tasks). Selecting cells.
The epic is a contract-heavy crash-restore hardening effort where a wrong routing-down risks corrupting identity/projection contracts, so I keep the higher-capability model throughout and reserve xhigh for the contract- and blast-radius-heavy tasks, dropping to high for the genuinely bounded single-surface ones.
Cells: .1 xhigh/opus 0.82; .2 xhigh/opus 0.85; .3 xhigh/opus 0.80; .4 xhigh/opus 0.72; .5 xhigh/opus 0.86; .6 high/opus 0.68; .7 high/opus 0.70; .8 high/opus 0.66.
